title: European Commission Publishes AI Act Proposal
description: The European Commission proposed the first-ever legal framework on AI, proposing a risk-based approach to regulating artificial intelligence systems.

title: EU Council Adopts General Approach
description: The Council of the EU agreed on its negotiating position (general approach) on the AI Act, adding provisions on general-purpose AI.

title: European Parliament Adopts Negotiating Position
description: MEPs adopted the Parliament's negotiating position on the AI Act, adding bans on biometric surveillance and emotion recognition.

title: Trilogue Agreement Reached
description: The European Parliament, Council, and Commission reached a provisional political agreement on the AI Act after marathon negotiations.

title: European Parliament Adopts AI Act
description: The European Parliament formally adopted the AI Act with 523 votes in favor, establishing the world's first comprehensive AI regulation.

title: Council of the EU Formally Adopts AI Act
description: The Council unanimously adopted the AI Act, completing the legislative process.

title: AI Act Enters into Force
description: The AI Act was published in the Official Journal and entered into force, starting the phased implementation timeline.

title: Prohibited AI Practices Take Effect
description: First tranche: AI systems posing unacceptable risks are banned, including social scoring, manipulative AI, and certain biometric categorization.

title: GPAI Model Obligations Apply
description: Obligations for general-purpose AI model providers take effect, including transparency, documentation, and copyright compliance.

title: Full Implementation — High-Risk AI Obligations
description: Complete implementation of the AI Act including all high-risk AI system requirements, conformity assessments, and enforcement powers.
